title: User Activation Physics

content.statement: Users don't stay because they signed up, they stay because they felt value fast.
content.question: What's your business's "aha moment," and how fast does a new user hit it? Can you make it faster?
content.equation: UAP = Activation Rate × (1 ÷ Time-to-Aha) × First-Session Value
sop: Define your specific aha moment, the exact instant a new user first gets real value from you.
Calculate your Activation Rate, the percentage of new users who actually reach that moment (see calculation, page 6).
Calculate your average Time-to-Aha, how long, in minutes or days, it takes them to get there (see calculation, page 6).
Rate First-Session Value 1-10, how strong that first experience feels once they arrive (see calculation, page 6).
Remove one unnecessary step between signup and the aha moment this month, and re-measure Time-to-Aha.
content.explainer: Every day a new user goes without their "aha" moment raises the probability they churn.
questions: What's your business's "aha moment," and how fast does a new user hit it? Can you make it faster?
What percentage of new customers actually reach your product's core value, versus signing up and quietly drifting away?
If you cut the time it takes a new user to feel your product's value in half, what would you have to remove or simplify?

definitions.def: The moment a new user reaches their first meaningful win in a product.
The first time a user gets real value from a product and "gets it."

content.equation_example: Activation Rate 40%, Time-to-Aha 3 days, First-Session Value rated 7 out of 10. 0.40 times (1 divided by 3) times 7 equals 0.93. Higher means more users are reaching real value, fast, and it's a strong first experience. Lower means users are signing up but drifting away before they ever feel why you're worth staying for.
content.closing_statement: Time to first value predicts whether someone stays.

caption:
Principle Nº 81: User Activation Physics

Users don't stay because they signed up, they stay because they felt value fast. Every day a new user goes without their "aha" moment raises the probability they churn.

Ask yourself: What's your business's "aha moment," and how fast does a new user hit it? Can you make it faster?
